document.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide

root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide
root.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1.2 Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide. It states the document is a business-level overview, not a detailed user manual, for how Spend Down Medical Assistance works in Cúram. Key topics include Spend Down concepts, medically needy groups, period and budget-period rules, retroactive coverage, allowed medical expense and Pay-In evidence, eligibility checking, creation and management of Spend Down product delivery cases, reassessment after evidence changes, overpayment/potential eligibility handling, renewal limitations, and manual case closure. Also includes notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory notice stating that, before using the information or the supported product, readers should review the Notices section on page 23. Includes Merative US L.P. copyright information for 2012 and 2024.

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Front matter for the Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide, applying to Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2. It includes the document purpose, audience, and references, then introduces Spend Down concepts: medically needy eligibility, spend down and budget periods, retroactive coverage, covered groups, and medical expense/pay-in evidence rules. It also outlines eligibility checking, product delivery creation, spend down details, coverage start date logic, and assigning expenses/pay-ins toward the spend down amount.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-22
children.summary: Covers Spend Down expense/pay-in handling and lifecycle rules. Explains assigning, modifying, undoing, and unassigning expenses/pay-ins, including validity, availability, member eligibility, classification, and sort order. Describes approving or denying a Spend Down period and resulting status/product delivery updates. Details reassessment after changes in circumstance, including recalculation when the Spend Down amount increases, decreases, or leads to overpayment or potential eligibility, plus retain/not retain coverage paths. Ends with ineligible reassessment, no renewal support, and manual product delivery closure.

Front matter and overview for the Cúram Spend Down Medical Assistance guide (8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2). Defines Spend Down, medically needy groups, period and budget-period concepts, retroactive coverage, and medical expense/pay-in evidence rules. Describes eligibility checking and Spend Down product delivery processing: creating details, assigning expenses/pay-ins, approving or denying periods, and updating coverage start/status. Also covers reassessment after changes, including recalculating spend down, retain/not retain coverage, overpayment or potential-eligibility handling, ineligible results, no renewal support, and manual case closure.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: Contains legal and copyright notices: fictitious example data disclaimer, sample program “AS IS” warranty/liability terms, a link to Merative’s privacy policy, and a list of trademarks owned by Merative and other companies. Useful if you need licensing, usage, privacy, or trademark information.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: Lists trademark and copyright notices for Merative documentation. It states that sample names in the examples are fictitious, sample programs are provided “as is” under the Merative Client Agreement, and includes the Merative privacy policy URL. The section identifies trademarks for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX, and notes that other names may belong to other owners.

Legal notice page covering reuse permissions for Merative publications. It allows personal noncommercial copying and limited internal enterprise use if proprietary notices are preserved, but forbids derivative works and external distribution without consent. It disclaims warranties, states the content is provided “AS IS,” reserves patent rights, warns about export-law compliance, and notes that non-Merative product and website references are informational only. Includes copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Contents page for the “Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide.” It lists the guide’s main sections: introduction, spend down program overview, eligibility, and case processing, with subtopics such as spend down period, budget period, retroactive coverage, rules, reassessment, eligibility renewal, and closing the product delivery case. Also includes front matter references (note, edition), notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: 1 Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide
1.1 Introduction
Document Purpose
children.summary: This section explains the document’s purpose, audience, and related references for the Spend Down Medical Assistance guide. It says the guide provides a business-level overview of the Spend Down program and its implementation in Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance, not detailed application instructions. It is intended for business users wanting to understand the coverage type, recorded evidence, eligibility checking, and product delivery management. It also points readers to supporting Medical Assistance documentation and related guides for Long Term Care and CHIP.

children.title: Audience
children.summary: Intended for business users, this section explains that the guide provides a business-level overview of the Spend Down Medical Assistance program and its Cúram implementation. It clarifies that the document is not a full application user guide, but covers helpful application specifics, the evidence used to determine Spend Down eligibility, and the processes for checking coverage eligibility and managing Spend Down product delivery. It also points readers to related Medical Assistance documentation, including the main Program Guide and guides for Long Term Care and CHIP.

children.title: Available Documentation
children.summary: This section introduces the Cúram Income Support for Medical Assistance Spend Down Guide and defines Spend Down as medical assistance for people whose income exceeds medically needy thresholds until medical expenses are considered. It states the document’s purpose is a business-level overview of the Spend Down program and its Cúram implementation, not a detailed user guide. It identifies the audience as business users and points them to related documentation, especially the Medical Assistance Program Guide, plus Long Term Care and Children’s Health Insurance Program guides.

Introduces the Spend Down Medical Assistance guide as a business-level overview of the program and its Cúram Income Support implementation. It explains that Spend Down applies when income is above medically needy thresholds but qualifies after medical expenses are considered. The section states the guide is not a detailed user manual, is intended for business users, and covers program evidence, eligibility checking, and product delivery management. It also points to related Medical Assistance documentation, including the main Program Guide, Long Term Care, and CHIP guides.

children.title: 1.2 Spend Down Medical Assistance Program Overview
Spend Down Overview
children.summary: Explains Spend Down for Medical Assistance/Medically Needy eligibility: when income exceeds state medically needy limits, the excess becomes the Spend Down amount, recalculated each period. Individuals meet it through allowed medical expenses and, in some states, Pay-In cash. Coverage starts from the date Spend Down is met through the end of the period. Notes it applies only to certain groups—pregnant women, children under 18, long-term care—and varies by state, including spend down and budget period rules.

children.title: Spend Down Period
children.summary: Explains the Spend Down period in Medical Assistance for medically needy individuals. It defines Spend Down as the excess income over a program’s eligibility limit, reduced by allowable medical expenses and sometimes cash Pay-In. Coverage begins on the date the amount is met and continues through the end of the spend down period, which is re-determined each period. Also describes the budget period used to calculate excess income. Includes covered groups such as pregnant women, children, and long-term care recipients, with state-specific rules.

children.title: Budget Period
children.summary: Explains the budget period for Spend Down/medically needy Medical Assistance. The budget period is the time span over which income is calculated to determine how much it exceeds the state eligibility standard, which becomes the Spend Down amount. This section is part of the broader Spend Down process, where eligibility and amount are reassessed each period, and coverage begins once the amount is met through medical expenses or pay-in.

children.title: Retroactive Coverage
children.summary: Explains retroactive Medical Assistance coverage under Spend Down: once an individual is eligible and has met the Spend Down for the budget period, they may receive up to 3 months of retroactive coverage. The section sits within broader Spend Down processing, including budget period rules and medical expense evidence handling. Useful for understanding when retroactive coverage applies and how it relates to meeting the Spend Down amount.

children.title: Coverage
children.summary: This section outlines Spend Down coverage processing after a case is created. It explains how an individual qualifies for Medical Assistance by meeting the Spend Down amount through medical expenses or pay-ins, how coverage continues for the rest of the budget period, and how retroactive coverage can apply for up to 3 months once eligibility is established. It also introduces Spend Down Medical Expense Evidence, including required fields, automatic validation/classification, and caseworker override behavior.

children.title: Spending Down
Spend Down Medical Expense Evidence
children.summary: Explains how medical expense evidence is used to meet a Spend Down in Cúram. The system validates, classifies, and sorts expenses, but only after the caseworker enters required details such as who received the service, whether it was medically necessary, Medicaid-covered, third-party liability, HMO status, and whether the service is state-covered. Classification may be set manually or automatically generated and later overwritten. Also notes budget-period behavior and retroactive coverage eligibility.

children.title: Pay-In Evidence
children.summary: Explains Pay-In evidence for Medically Needy Spend Down cases. Pay-In is a household payment to state authorities used alone or with certain expenses to meet Spend Down, and it is configured for Medically Needy Children/Pregnant Women with Spend Down. Capturing, updating, or deleting a Pay-In creates and maintains related Financial module Payment Received records when financial generation is enabled. Also introduces Spend Down eligibility: eligibility rules, checking decisions, automatically calculated Spend Down amounts, and period-based redetermination until Spend Down is met.

This section explains how Spending Down works in Cúram: an individual can meet the Spend Down using allowed medical expenses or a pay-in, and once the amount is met, additional medical costs may be covered for the rest of the budget period. It also notes budget periods are typically one month by default, multiple budget periods within one Spend Down period are not supported, and up to 3 months of retroactive coverage may apply after eligibility. It introduces medical expense evidence validation/classification and required caseworker inputs.

Overview of Spend Down Medical Assistance in Cúram. Explains that people whose income exceeds medically needy limits can qualify by meeting a calculated Spend Down amount through allowed medical expenses and, in some states, Pay-In cash. Eligibility and the amount are redetermined each period; coverage starts when the amount is met and lasts through the rest of the spend down period, with up to 3 months retroactive coverage. Covers eligible groups, default one-month budget period, and medical expense evidence rules/classification for caseworker entry.

children.title: 1.3 Spend Down Eligibility
Introduction
children.summary: Introduces Pay-In evidence and Spend Down eligibility in Cúram 8.1.2. Pay-In refers to household payments used to help meet Spend Down when income is slightly above Medically Needy limits; it is configured for Medically Needy Children and Pregnant Women with Spend Down. Captured Pay-Ins create household records and, if Financials are enabled, Payment Received records that update automatically on edits. The chapter then outlines Spend Down eligibility rules, periodic re-determination, and caseworker check-eligibility functions and decision viewing.

children.title: Eligibility Determination
children.summary: Explains how Spend Down eligibility is determined in Cúram. Eligibility is based on evidence and rules for Medically Needy Medical Assistance: the person must first qualify on all categories except income, then meet the Spend Down amount for the budget period. It also outlines check eligibility functions for caseworkers, including viewing eligible/ineligible decisions and the automatically calculated Spend Down amount. Eligibility and Spend Down must be reassessed each period, and coverage applies from the date Spend Down is met through the end of that period.

children.title: Check Eligibility
children.summary: Summarizes check eligibility for Spend Down Medical Assistance. Caseworkers can run eligibility rules for medical assistance, including Spend Down programs, view eligible/ineligible decisions, and see the automatically calculated Spend Down amount. Eligibility is reassessed each period, and once the Spend Down amount is met, coverage applies from that date through the end of the period.

children.title: Rules
children.summary: This section explains how Cúram applies business rules to determine Medical Assistance with Spend Down eligibility from captured evidence. It describes what happens after eligibility is found: caseworkers can authorize, decline, or deny results, and the system creates or updates a Spend Down product delivery and details record. It also covers recording medical expenses or pay-ins, classifying expenses for use against a Spend Down period, and viewing details across periods. Retroactive eligibility is limited to up to 3 months, with each month treated as a separate Spend Down period.

children.title: Spend Down Details
children.summary: Explains how Spend Down eligibility is handled in Cúram: business rules determine Medical Assistance with Spend Down eligibility, and caseworkers can authorize, decline, or deny results. When authorized, the system creates or updates a Spend Down product delivery and records Spend Down details for each period. It also covers entering medical expense or pay-in evidence, classifying and ordering usable expenses, viewing details across periods, and retroactive eligibility rules, including up to 3 months of separate retroactive Spend Down periods.

children.title: Retroactive Eligibility
children.summary: Explains retroactive coverage for Medical Assistance with Spend Down. An individual may receive up to 3 months of retroactive coverage after being determined eligible and meeting the Spend Down amount for the budget period. Each retroactive month is treated as a separate Spend Down period, and the person must meet both coverage eligibility and Spend Down requirements for each month. For more detail, it refers readers to the Medical Assistance Program Guide.

Introduces Spend Down eligibility for Medically Needy Medical Assistance in Cúram. It explains that eligibility is determined from recorded evidence against Spend Down rules: the person must first qualify on all non-income criteria, then meet the Spend Down amount for the budget period. Caseworkers can run check eligibility, view eligible/ineligible decisions, and see the calculated Spend Down amount. Eligibility and Spend Down are reassessed each period, and coverage begins on the date the amount is met through period end.

children.title: 1.4 Spend Down Case Processing
Introduction
children.summary: Introduces Cúram Spend Down rules for Medical Assistance: eligibility is based on evidence and Medically Needy coverage rules. After eligibility, a caseworker can authorize, decline, or deny results; authorizing creates or updates a Spend Down product delivery and its details record. The section explains recording medical expenses or pay-ins, classifying usable expenses, and viewing all Spend Down periods for a household member. It also notes retroactive eligibility of up to 3 months, with each retroactive month treated as a separate Spend Down period.

children.title: Spend Down Details
Spend Down Coverage Start Date
children.summary: Explains how Spend Down coverage start date is determined from application date, Spend Down period dates, and the date of the last evidence item used to reduce the outstanding amount to zero. Different rules apply when application is before or after the period end date, and the evidence date used is either the incurred date for Medical Expenses or the paid date for Pay-Ins. Also notes that evidence must be activated before assignment and that selected Expenses/Pay-Ins are ordered in 10-point increments.

Covers the Spend Down Details page and what caseworkers can view for each period: dates, amount, outstanding amount, status, whether met, reason, and period status. Explains how the Spend Down coverage start date is calculated from the application date, period dates, and the last evidence item used, with rules varying when the application date is before or after the period end date. Also describes assigning Expenses/Pay-Ins toward Spend Down, required activation, and 10-point ordering increments.

children.title: Assigning towards Spend Down
Order Expenses/Pay-In Assigned
children.summary: Explains how Spend Down expenses and pay-ins are assigned and ordered. The system predefines a suggested application order, and when a caseworker selects an Expense/Pay-In for a Spend Down period, it receives an order number starting at 10 and increasing by 10 for each additional item. Useful for understanding how evidence is prioritized and tracked during Spend Down allocation.

children.title: Assigning Expenses
children.summary: Explains how caseworkers assign medical expenses or pay-ins toward a Spend Down in the Assign Expenses wizard. It shows available outstanding Spend Down, total usable amount, and default assign values, then lets the user select one or more expenses and amounts. The system validates, classifies, and orders expenses, applies highest-priority items first, and caps assignments at the outstanding amount. It also covers single pay-in selection, oldest-first ordering, and the rules for valid expenses.

children.title: Assigning Pay-In
children.summary: This section explains how caseworkers assign Spend Down amounts from expenses and Pay-Ins. It covers editing assigned items and changing their application order, with lowest order number applied first. For expenses, the wizard shows outstanding Spend Down, total available amount, and default amounts; caseworkers can select one or more expenses per transaction, and the system validates, classifies, orders, and adjusts amounts automatically. For Pay-Ins, only one can be selected per transaction, and available Pay-Ins are listed oldest first.

children.title: Spend Down Expense/Pay-In Rules
Valid Expense
children.summary: This section explains how the system determines whether a medical expense is valid for Spend Down use. A valid expense must be allowable, within the expiry time limit, medically necessary, not on hold, have an available amount, be available for Spend Down, and be incurred by a valid household member. It also notes that allowable medical expenses are defined in decision tables in the implemented rule set.

children.title: Available Amount
children.summary: Defines how to calculate the “available amount” of a medical expense for Spend Down: start with the obligated amount, subtract amounts already applied in other Spend Down periods, and use the remaining balance. It also states when an expense is available for use (unpaid, part paid, or paid in full in the application or retroactive month), who counts as a valid member, and how medical expenses are classified as incurred or deductible, including when a classification must be determined.

children.title: Available for Use
children.summary: This section defines when a medical expense is “available for use” toward Spend Down. It explains how to calculate the available amount by taking the obligated amount and subtracting amounts already applied in other Spend Down periods. It also states eligibility rules for expenses (unpaid, part paid, or paid in full in the application or retroactive month), identifies valid members whose expenses may count, and outlines how medical expenses are classified as incurred or deductible.

children.title: Valid Member
children.summary: Defines who counts as a valid member for spend down medical expenses: the client or a family member who either receives Medical Assistance, is disqualified from it, or deems to the client, and who lives at home with the person responsible for paying the expense. This section helps determine whether an expense can be considered for spend down based on member eligibility.

children.title: Classification
children.summary: Defines how medical expenses are classified for Spend Down processing. It states that expenses must be categorized as incurred or deductible, and unclassified expenses are assigned one of these based on criteria. The section also outlines related eligibility concepts: available amount, available-for-use rules, and valid member requirements. It begins the definition of “incurred” as an expense for a service that would be covered by Medical Assistance.

children.title: Incurred
children.summary: Defines how to classify medical expenses as incurred versus deductible, and when an expense counts as incurred. It also covers related spend-down rules: how to calculate available amount, what makes an expense available for use, and who qualifies as a valid member. The incurred definition begins here: an expense is incurred when it was for a service that would be covered by Medical Assistance.

children.title: Deductible
children.summary: Defines when a medical expense is deductible for Spend Down: it must be medically necessary, incurred when the person was not eligible for Medical Assistance, and for services not payable by Medical Assistance. Also covers sort order for applying expenses (paid expenses oldest first; unpaid deductible expenses oldest incurred first), plus caseworker actions to modify or undo applied Expense/Pay-In amounts, including validation rules and how records are updated or superseded based on Spend Down status.

children.title: Sort Order
children.summary: Explains Spend Down sort order for valid medical expenses: paid expenses are applied first by oldest paid date, then unpaid deductible expenses by oldest incurred date. Notes that unavailable expenses are stored but hidden from the caseworker. Also covers caseworker actions to modify or undo applied Expense/Pay-In entries, including validations, and how records are updated or superseded depending on Spend Down status (Not Approved vs Approved).

Defines Spend Down expense/pay-in processing rules. It explains how caseworkers assign expenses or pay-ins, how the system validates and orders them, and how excess selected amounts are adjusted. Key criteria include valid expense rules, available amount calculation, when an expense is available for use, valid member eligibility, and classification as incurred or deductible. It also starts the incurred definition: a service covered by Medical Assistance. Open this node for the core rules governing whether and how medical expenses/pay-ins count toward Spend Down.

children.title: Modify Applied Expense/Pay-In
children.summary: Explains how a caseworker can modify an applied Expense/Pay-In in a Spend Down period, including changing the applied amount or order number. Notes validation limits: amount cannot exceed the outstanding amount or available amount. Describes record behavior by status: in Not Approved periods, the existing Spend Down Details record is updated; in Approved periods, it is superseded and a new record is created. Also covers updating amount used/available and adjusting remaining outstanding amounts when needed.

children.title: Undo Applied Expense/Pay-In
children.summary: Explains how a caseworker can undo a previously applied Expense/Pay-In on a Spend Down period. For Not Approved periods, the current Spend Down Details record is updated and the Expense/Pay-In is removed from Applied Expenses/Pay-Ins; for Approved periods, the record is superseded and a new record is created. If undoing leaves an outstanding amount greater than zero, the Spend Down details must be adjusted accordingly.

children.title: Unassign All
children.summary: Describes Spend Down actions around applied evidence: Unassign All removes previously assigned Expense evidence in one request and updates the outstanding amount, with affected Spend Down detail records superseded/updated and evidence made available for reapplication. Also covers approving a Spend Down period when the amount reaches zero, which sets status to Approved and updates related fields, and denying a period, which sets status to Denied and reverses applied amounts. Ends with the start of reassessment handling after changes in circumstance.

children.title: Approve Spend Down Period
children.summary: This section explains how a caseworker approves a Spend Down period once the outstanding amount reaches zero. It covers system validations, automatic updates to Spend Down status, outstanding amount, product delivery status, reason, coverage start date, and expense/pay-in usage. The surrounding text also describes unassigning applied evidence, denying a Spend Down period, and reassessment when circumstances change. Open this node for the workflow and system effects of approving, denying, or adjusting Spend Down periods.

children.title: Deny Spend Down Period
children.summary: Explains Spend Down period actions in Cúram: unassigning previously applied Expense evidence, approving a period when the spend down reaches zero, and denying a period when expenses/pay-ins are insufficient or the client declines. Denial requires a reason, sets the period status to Denied, may close the product delivery for the current period, and reverses applied amounts. Also notes that reassessment can recalculate Spend Down after a change in circumstance.

Covers how caseworkers assign medical expenses and Pay-Ins toward a Spend Down period, including viewing outstanding/available amounts, default assignment values, and evidence activation requirements. Explains system ordering rules (initial suggested order, order numbers starting at 10, lowest order applied first), expense validation/classification, available amount and valid member criteria, and sort order rules. Also describes modifying or undoing applied items, with different behavior for Not Approved vs Approved periods and record supersession/update rules.

children.title: Reassessment
Change in Circumstance
Recalculate the Spend Down Amount
children.summary: Explains how Spend Down amounts are recalculated when income, expense, or household evidence changes and the household no longer qualifies for another coverage type. If the new amount is higher than the old and the old spend down was met, the system supersedes the prior details, resets outstanding amount/not met status, and may reset the product delivery case to Approved in certain cases. Also covers caseworker options to retain coverage by applying existing or new Expense/Pay-In evidence, including undoing prior applications for past periods.

children.title: New Spend Down Amount greater than old Spend Down Amount
children.summary: Explains what happens when a reassessment increases a period’s Spend Down amount after a change in income, expenses, or household evidence. If the old amount was already met, the system supersedes the current Spend Down Details, resets outstanding amount and met status, and may reset the product delivery case to Approved depending on period/status. Caseworkers then decide whether to retain coverage by adding or reapplying Expense/Pay-In evidence to meet the new amount, especially for past periods.

children.title: Retain Coverage after recalculation of Spend Down amount
children.summary: Explains what happens when a Spend Down amount is recalculated after a change in evidence and the caseworker wants to retain coverage. If the new amount is higher, the member must meet the new Outstanding Spend Down amount using existing or new Expense/Pay-In evidence. For past periods, previously applied Expense/Pay-In evidence must be undone from the start of the impacted period so it reapplies correctly. Caseworkers can review the current Spend Down Details record and apply evidence toward the Spend Down.

children.title: Not Retain Coverage after recalculation of Spend Down amount
children.summary: Explains what happens when a recalculated Spend Down amount is not retained for coverage. The caseworker denies the period, selects a denial reason, and unassigns expenses/pay-ins so they can be reused later. The period status becomes “Not Approved,” and expense used/available amounts are updated. If it is the current period with no future applications, the standard close case function must be triggered. Also introduces consequences of a lower recalculated Spend Down amount: overpayment handling and possible reapplication for past-period eligibility.

children.title: New Spend Down Amount less than old Spend Down Amount
children.summary: Explains what happens when a recalculated Spend Down amount is lower than the original. Two cases are covered: if the period was already met, the household may have overpaid and the system updates the Spend Down Details with a negative outstanding amount; if it was not met, the member may have been able to meet the lower amount, so the current record is superseded and the household may reapply for the past period to assess potential eligibility.

children.title: Over Payment due to Recalculation of Spend Down
children.summary: Explains what happens when a Spend Down amount is recalculated downward or coverage is not retained. If the new amount is met, the system approves the period, marks status Approved, sets reason “Spend Down Met,” updates coverage dates and expense usage, and may activate the case. If coverage is denied, status becomes Not Approved and expenses are unassigned. For reduced amounts, the section covers overpayment handling via a superseded Spend Down Details record with a negative outstanding amount, and potential eligibility when a lower amount might have been met.

children.title: Potential Eligibility for Medical Assistance Program with Spend Down due to Recalculation of Spend Down
children.summary: Explains actions after Spend Down recalculation for a Medicaid assistance case. If the recalculated amount reaches zero and coverage is retained, the system approves the period, marks it “Spend Down Met,” updates expenses and coverage start date, and may activate the case. If coverage is not retained, the period is denied/not approved and expenses are unassigned. Also covers lower recalculated amounts: overpayments when the higher amount was already met, or potential eligibility when the member may have met the new lower amount, requiring an option to reapply for a past period.

children.title: Ineligible after Reassessment
children.summary: Explains what happens when a reassessment makes a previously eligible Spend Down period ineligible: the caseworker closes the product delivery. Notes that the current Spend Down implementation does not support eligibility renewal, so household members must reapply for a later period. Also describes how a Spend Down product delivery case can be closed at any point via the close link, which sets the case status to closed and sends the appropriate template-based communication.

Covers how Spend Down cases are reassessed when income, expense, or household evidence changes. If the recalculated amount is higher, the prior record may be superseded, met/not met status reset, and coverage either retained by applying more Expense/Pay-In evidence or denied with expenses unassigned and case closure if needed. If lower, it addresses overpayment or possible past-period eligibility, including reapplication. Also notes current vs past period handling and when product delivery case status changes to Approved, Active, or Not Approved.

Explains Spend Down reassessment after changes in income, expenses, or household composition. Reassessment applies to current or past periods and can recalculate the amount higher or lower. If higher, the record is superseded, met/not met status resets, and the caseworker may retain coverage by adding/redistributing Expense/Pay-In evidence or deny the period, unassigning expenses and possibly closing the case. If lower, it addresses overpayment or possible past-period eligibility and may require reapplication. Coverage/product delivery statuses may shift to Approved, Active, Closed, or Not Approved.

children.title: Eligibility Renewal
children.summary: The Spend Down program does not currently support automatic eligibility renewal. If a household member needs coverage for a new period, the caseworker must use a manual process and have the claimant reapply for Spend Down. This section is useful for understanding how renewal requests are handled after an eligible period ends.

children.title: Closing Product Delivery Case
children.summary: Explains how to close a Spend Down product delivery case. A case can be closed at any point from the product delivery home page using the close link; submitting the request sets the case status to closed and sends the appropriate communication to the household member. Also notes that if reassessment makes a previously eligible Spend Down period ineligible, the caseworker closes the product delivery, and that eligibility renewal is not supported, so a new application is required for a later period.

Covers Spend Down case processing lifecycle: viewing Spend Down details for all periods, retroactive coverage rules, assigning Expenses/Pay-Ins, ordering and validation rules, and modifying applied evidence. Explains coverage start date calculations, available/valid expense criteria, and reassessment outcomes when the Spend Down amount changes. Also describes caseworker actions to retain or deny coverage after reassessment, unassign evidence, handle overpayments or possible past eligibility, and close the product delivery.

Business-level overview of Cúram Spend Down Medical Assistance. Explains how people with income above medically needy limits can qualify by meeting a calculated Spend Down amount using allowed medical expenses and, in some states, Pay-In cash. Covers program scope, covered groups, default one-month budget period, up to 3 months retroactive coverage, and medical expense/pay-in evidence entry and validation. Also outlines eligibility checking, product delivery creation, reassessment, retroactive coverage, overpayment/potential eligibility handling, and case closing; not a detailed user manual.
